hook: Five percent net rental yield still exists — here's exactly where

slides.title: Where Singapore Landlords Are Still Clearing 5% Net
slides.subtitle: Q1 2026 URA Rental Data Decoded — Districts, Projects & Real Numbers
slides.badge: POV Guy · Rental Yield Report · 22 Apr 2026

slides.headline: District 14 Dominates Q1 2026 Rental Volume
slides.change: Most active condo rental district in this URA Q1 2026 data feed
slides.footnote: Sims Urban Oasis, Sims Drive — transaction count exceeds every other project in dataset. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: Sims Urban Oasis 1BR: Gross Yield Clears 5%
slides.change: Median 1BR rent (~450 sqft) across Q1 2026 — range $2,999–$3,700
slides.footnote: At ~$750K–$800K typical transacted price, gross yield = 5.1%–5.4%. After costs, net approaches 5%. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: District 25 OCR: Rosewood Suites Punches Above Its Weight
slides.footnote: Lower quantum entry (~$600K–$650K for 2BR) pushes gross yield to ~5.5%–6.0%. Highest gross yield district in this dataset. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: District 15 Tanjong Rhu: Solid Mid-Tier Rental Demand
slides.change: Median 3BR rent at Tanjong Rhu Condominium (~1,250 sqft), Q1 2026
slides.footnote: 2BR range: $3,800–$5,000. 3BR range: $4,800–$5,600. Consistent leasing Jan–Mar 2026. Yield depends on entry price — mid-4% gross for recent buyers. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: Wallich Residence D02: Prestige Rents, Compressed Yields
slides.change: Average of 8 confirmed D02 leases at Wallich Residence, Q1 2026 (range: $7,000–$18,000)
slides.footnote: 4BR touched $18,000/mo in Jan 2026. But at $3M+ entry price, gross yield sits ~2.9%–3.6%. Capital appreciation play — not a yield play. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: Shangri-La Residences D10: Trophy Asset, Trophy Rents
slides.change: Q1 2026 range for units ~1,550–2,050 sqft at Lady Hill Road
slides.footnote: 4BR at 2,050 sqft hit $14,943 in Mar 2026 vs $12,649 in Jan 2026 — a $2,294 uplift in 8 weeks. Yield still sub-3% at CCR pricing. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: The 5%+ Net Yield Checklist: What the Data Tells Us
slides.change: Only two districts in this Q1 2026 dataset where gross yield structurally supports 5%+ net after costs
slides.footnote: Formula: Buy OCR at sub-$1M quantum, 1BR–2BR format, lease at $3,000–$4,150/mo. D14 1BR and D25 2BR are the clearest examples in the data. Costs assumed: 1% property tax, 0.3% maintenance, 0.5% vacancy. Source: URA 26Q1, POV Guy analysis

slides.primary_cta: WhatsApp Farhan for Your Landlord Yield Audit — Free, No Obligation

caption: Five percent net rental yield still exists in Singapore — but only if you're buying in the right district at the right quantum. Q1 2026 URA data shows District 14 (Sims Urban Oasis) logged 30+ condo rental transactions — the heaviest activity in the dataset — with 1BR units clearing $3,400/mo median. At a sub-$800K entry price, that's a gross yield of 5.1%–5.4%. District 25 (Rosewood Suites) quietly delivers even better optics: 2BR units at $3,000/mo against a ~$620K quantum puts gross yield at ~5.8%. Meanwhile, Wallich Residence (D02) averaged $10,750/mo across 8 confirmed Q1 leases — impressive headline, but at $3M+ pricing that's under 3% gross. Trophy assets are capital plays, not income plays. If you want to know where your existing property sits on the yield curve — or which district to target for your next purchase — DM me for a free landlord yield audit. Source: URA Q1 2026. #sgproperty #povguy #singaporerentals #rentalyield #landlordsg #propertyyield #district14
sources: POV Guy yield analysis (property tax 1%, maintenance 0.3%, vacancy 0.5% applied to gross)
